title: rubus hedycarpus subsp. portuensis
intro: Rubus hedycarpus subsp. portuensis, also known as the Port Phillip bramble, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family, native to southeastern Australia. The leaves and fruit of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which aid in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Rubus hedycarpus subsp. portuensis have been associated with reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as teas and infusions, have been used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throat.
